description: §12.4 — a single function whose body accesses BOTH a server-only resource (a ?{} SQL context) AND a client-only DOM global (`document.title`) is unplaceable: route analysis can assign it to neither side. It fires E-ROUTE-005 (error) — DISTINCT from E-ROUTE-002 (a server fn calling a separate client-only fn). Guards the S263-review hole where this shape silently shipped the DOM code into the server bundle.
rationale: §12.4: a single function accessing both a server-only resource and a client-only DOM global cannot be placed on either side (a server context has no `document`; a client context cannot reach the server-only resource). The compiler SHALL emit E-ROUTE-005 rather than silently place it server-side. It is NOT the E-ROUTE-002 cross-call shape, so E-ROUTE-002 does not fire.
